name: Seven Sisters Cliffs (Seaford to Eastbourne)
details: 14 miles (22.5km) coastal hike with rolling chalk cliffs and ~600m elevation. Very scenic, classic free outdoor trail highly recommended.

name: Box Hill & Mole Gap Trail
details: 10 miles (16km) loop in the Surrey Hills AONB. Steep ascent to Box Hill summit offering stunning views of the North Downs. A frequent favourite.
search_query: Box Hill & Westhumble Railway Station, UK

name: South Downs Way (Hassocks to Lewes)
details: 11 miles (18km) linear walk via Ditchling Beacon with ~500m elevation. Incredible ridge views. Free outdoor activity.

name: Epping Forest (Oak Trail)
details: 7 miles (11km) moderate woodland and field trail past the ancient Deer Sanctuary. Quick and rewarding free outdoor activity.

name: Chilterns (Henley to Stonor Park)
details: 12 miles (19km) through the Chiltern Hills AONB woodlands and rolling valleys. Excellent elevation for conditioning.

name: Ashridge Estate (Tring)
details: 10 miles (16km) woodland hike on the Ivinghoe Beacon ridge with fantastic panoramic views over the surrounding counties.

name: White Cliffs of Dover
details: 10 miles (16km) spectacular coastal path from Dover to Deal via South Foreland Lighthouse. Challenging cliffside walk.

name: Chess Valley Walk
details: 10 miles (16km) following the River Chess through the Chilterns from Rickmansworth to Chesham. Gentle but highly scenic.

name: Hastings to Rye (Coastal)
details: 13 miles (21km) undulating coastal hike passing the rugged cliffs of Hastings Country Park and the historic town of Winchelsea.

name: Cotswolds (Moreton-in-Marsh)
details: 12 miles (19km) gentle but long hike through quintessential English villages along the Monarch's Way to Bourton-on-the-Water.

name: Bath Spa
details: Explore the historic Roman Baths, Bath Abbey, and the Royal Crescent. Excellent Georgian architecture and city walking.

name: Oxford
details: Historic university city. Visit the Bodleian Library, Ashmolean Museum, and enjoy extensive free walking routes along the River Thames.

name: Cambridge
details: Explore the University colleges, enjoy punting on the River Cam, and visit the Fitzwilliam Museum. Rich in history and Gothic architecture.

name: South Downs Way (Amberley to Devil's Dyke)
details: 22 miles (35km) across two days with steep rolling hills and valley views. Accommodation: Tent camping at local farm campsites (£15/night) or budget beds at YHA Truleigh Hill.

name: Ouse Valley Viaduct & Ardingly Reservoir
details: 9 miles (14.5km) circular from Balcombe. Famous Instagram spot for the symmetric viaduct arches and peaceful reservoir.

name: Margate to Broadstairs (Viking Coastal Trail)
details: 7 miles (11km) coastal walk. Highly scenic chalk stacks at Botany Bay and historic Victorian seaside towns.

name: Goring Gap & Thames Path
details: 10 miles (16km) following the Thames where it cuts through the Chilterns. Very scenic, flat but long riverside walk to Pangbourne.

name: New Forest (Brockenhurst to Tall Trees Trail)
details: 11 miles (18km) woodland trail through ancient redwoods and open heathland with wild ponies.

name: Wye Valley (Chepstow to Tintern Abbey)
details: 10 miles (16km) challenging climb to the Eagle's Nest viewpoint overlooking the Wye River, ending at the majestic Tintern Abbey ruins.

name: Surrey Hills (Leith Hill & Holmbury Hill)
details: 10 miles (16km) challenging loop hitting the highest point in South East England (Leith Hill Tower) with expansive views to the English Channel.

name: Rye to Camber Sands
details: 8 miles (13km) walk from the cobbled streets of medieval Rye across marshes to the massive sand dunes of Camber.

name: Canterbury
details: Historic pilgrimage city. Visit the UNESCO World Heritage Canterbury Cathedral, St Augustine's Abbey, and wander the medieval streets.

name: Brighton & Hove
details: Vibrant coastal city. Visit the exotic Royal Pavilion, the historic Lanes for independent shops, and walk the vibrant seafront promenade.

name: Stratford-upon-Avon
details: Birthplace of William Shakespeare. Explore historic Tudor homes, the Royal Shakespeare Theatre, and enjoy scenic walks along the River Avon.

name: Windsor & Eton
details: Home to Windsor Castle (the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world) and Eton College. Walk the Long Walk in Windsor Great Park.
search_query: Windsor & Eton Riverside Railway Station, UK

name: Isle of Wight (Needles Coastal Path)
details: 20 miles (32km) coastal trek from Yarmouth to the dramatic chalk stacks of The Needles. Accommodation: Budget camping at Stoats Farm (£15/night).

name: South Downs (Winchester to Exton)
details: 12 miles (19km) hiking the very start of the South Downs Way from the historic city of Winchester, ending in the Meon Valley.

name: Cotswolds (Kemble to Cirencester)
details: 10 miles (16km) walking from the official source of the River Thames through the Roman town of Cirencester and its massive amphitheatre earthworks.

name: North Downs Way (Guildford to Dorking)
details: 14 miles (22.5km) classic ridge hike through the Surrey Hills AONB, taking in Newlands Corner and St Martha's Hill.

name: Seaford Head to Cuckmere Haven
details: 6 miles (9.5km) hike offering the absolute best, most iconic viewpoint of the Seven Sisters cliffs across the Cuckmere River meanders.

name: Peak District (Kinder Scout via Edale)
details: 10 miles (16km) challenging scramble up Grindsbrook Clough to the highest point in the Peak District. Featured heavily as a must-do.

name: Peak District (Dark Peak - Bamford to Edale)
details: 20 miles (32km) across two days over the Great Ridge and Kinder Plateau. Accommodation: Tent camping is highly feasible. Fieldhead Campsite in Edale is an excellent, cheap official site (£15/night), or legal wild camping (bivvying) discretely on the high moorland.

name: Lake District (Windermere to Langdale)
details: 25 miles (40km) rugged mountain trekking including the Langdale Pikes. Accommodation: Great Langdale Campsite is available for budget tent camping (£20/night), or cheap YHA hostels in Ambleside/Langdale.

name: Snowdonia (Betws-y-Coed & Snowdon)
details: Challenging mountain ascent of Mount Snowdon via the Pyg Track. Accommodation: Tent camping is feasible at local sites near Betws-y-Coed (£15/night) or budget stays at the YHA Snowdon Pen-y-Pass.

name: Brecon Beacons (Abergavenny & Pen y Fan)
details: 15 miles (24km) challenging hike up the highest peak in South Wales. Accommodation: Tent camping is feasible at various farm sites near Abergavenny (£15/night) or wild camping high up in the National Park if discrete.

name: Eyam & Curbar Edge (Peak District)
details: 16 miles (25km) across two days. Historic plague village of Eyam and the dramatic gritstone escarpment of Curbar Edge. Accommodation: YHA Eyam (budget hostel) or legal wild camping discretely on the moors.

name: Dartmoor (Okehampton to Yes Tor)
details: 18 miles (29km) across rugged, remote moorland to the highest points in Dartmoor. Accommodation: Dartmoor is the only place in England where wild camping is legally permitted in designated zones. Free and highly recommended for experienced hikers.

name: Jurassic Coast (Weymouth to Lulworth Cove)
details: 14 miles (22.5km) strenuous coastal path featuring the iconic Durdle Door arch. Constant steep ascents and descents. Accommodation: Durdle Door Holiday Park has budget tent pitches (£20/night), or YHA Lulworth Cove.

name: Yorkshire Dales (Malham Cove)
details: 14 miles (22.5km) over two days. Features the spectacular 80m high curving limestone cliff of Malham Cove. Accommodation: YHA Malham offers budget beds, or pitch at Gordale Scar Campsite (£12/night).

name: Malvern Hills (End to End)
details: 9 miles (14.5km) intense ridge walk over all major peaks including Worcestershire Beacon. Challenging with ~800m elevation.
